steps.title: Identify what the verb requires
steps.content: The left side ends with the linking verb is, requiring a complement to complete the sentence. The right side is the noun phrase the chromophore, which fills that role.

steps.title: Apply the rule for verbs and complements
steps.content: Within an independent clause, a verb and its complement must not be separated by punctuation. Choice B correctly places no punctuation between them. The other choices all wrongly insert punctuation—commas, dashes, or semicolons—that breaks this necessary direct connection. That matches choice B — B is correct.
